correction_note: ckpt-0002's completed_tasks text named the decision-log entry as D-020; a technical review of PR #12 found D-020 is already reserved by open PR #8 (framework-v1 pack decision, head d2ba9bb4) on its own unmerged branch. The decision is renamed D-021 in this session's work (see completed_tasks below); ckpt-0001 and ckpt-0002's original text are left unedited per the append-only journal rule -- this checkpoint is the correction record, not an edit to them.

completed_tasks.task: renamed the M-GPI-1 decision from D-020 to D-021 across DECISION_LOG.md, docs/program/gateway/{architecture,rest-mapping}.md, pyproject.toml comment, CHANGELOG.md; verified via grep that D-020 does not appear anywhere in this branch and D-021 appears exactly where expected

completed_tasks.task: added tests/unit/test_decision_log.py: a duplicate-decision-identifier check over DECISION_LOG.md, plus an explicit pin that D-020 (reserved by PR #8) must never appear in this branch's file

added src/cavbench/gateway/bind.py: validate_loopback_host() rejects any bind address that resolves to a non-loopback interface (0.0.0.0, ::, LAN/public addresses, non-loopback-resolving or mixed-resolution hostnames) before any socket opens; wired into GatewayRestServer.__init__ (serve() delegates to it)
completed_tasks.evidence: src/cavbench/gateway/bind.py; src/cavbench/gateway/rest.py; NonLoopbackBindError added to errors.py

completed_tasks.task: added tests/unit/test_gateway_bind.py: 15 tests covering allowed (127.0.0.1/::1/localhost) and rejected (0.0.0.0, ::, LAN/public literals, non-loopback hostname via mocked getaddrinfo, mixed-resolution hostname, unresolvable hostname) cases, plus GatewayRestServer-level accept/reject tests

added capability enforcement to GatewaySession: _check_capability() verifies read namespace is scenario-visible and write/compensate tool_name+namespace matches an advertised operation from capabilities(), before any ToolFacade call; write and compensate tools are enforced as non-interchangeable (CapabilityViolationError, gateway-level 'capability_violation' rejection)
completed_tasks.evidence: src/cavbench/gateway/core.py: _write_operations/_compensate_operations/_readable_namespaces/_check_capability/_check_read_capability/_check_write_capability; wired into _dispatch() before any tools.* call

completed_tasks.task: added tests/contract/test_gateway_capability_enforcement.py: adversarial tests for unadvertised tool_name, valid tool wrong namespace, valid tool wrong action, compensation-as-write, write-as-compensation, arbitrary read namespace, unavailable scenario operation, plus positive tests for advertised operations -- every negative case asserts zero ToolFacade calls

fixed tests/contract/test_gateway_neutrality.py::test_compensate_action_maps_to_tool_facade_write_with_compensation_for, which had targeted ER-05's 'cancel-1' step (kind=write, not a real compensate step) -- now targets ER-04's genuine kind=='compensate' step ('release-1', tool release_inventory, compensates reserve-1)
completed_tasks.evidence: test passes under new capability enforcement

completed_tasks.task: fixed examples/reference_candidate/driver.py::ReferenceCandidate._write, which always sent action='write' even for a kind=='compensate' plan step (would now be rejected as compensation-tool-sent-as-write); now sends action='compensate' with parameters.compensation_for=step.compensates when the acting step's kind is 'compensate'
completed_tasks.evidence: ER-04 guarded hazard-pattern run still reaches commit_valid_success=True via a real compensate action (verified via examples/gateway_rest_demo.py --scenario ER-04 --mode guarded)

completed_tasks.task: added src/cavbench/gateway/capabilities.py: canonical capability model (OperationDescriptor, derive_operations, readable_resources, operations_by_action_and_tool), deduplicated by full (action, tool_name, namespace, resource_id) tuple, never by tool_name alone
completed_tasks.evidence: src/cavbench/gateway/capabilities.py; tests/unit/test_gateway_capabilities_model.py (6 tests, synthetic scenario proving distinct descriptors under different action/namespace/resource_id)

completed_tasks.task: rewired GatewaySession.capabilities() and _check_capability() to both read from the same memoized derive_operations() result, eliminating the two previously-separate tool-name-keyed lookups

extended capability enforcement to resource_id: write/compensate now require exact (tool_name, namespace, resource_id) match; read now requires (namespace, resource_id) to be scenario-visible (union of read/write/compensate targets, since a well-behaved candidate reads before acting)
completed_tasks.evidence: tests/contract/test_gateway_capability_enforcement.py: wrong-resource_id tests for write/compensate/read, a resource visible for one operation but not another (payment:P-4004 read+write-visible, not compensate-visible), exact positive cases

completed_tasks.task: removed the incorrect 'disjoint by construction' claim about write/compensate operations from code comments and docs; documented that non-interchangeability comes from full-descriptor matching, not the data model
completed_tasks.evidence: src/cavbench/gateway/capabilities.py module docstring; docs/program/gateway/architecture.md 'One canonical capability model' section
